dwarf.name: Dwarf

senses.name: Darkvision
senses.description: You can see in darkness and dim light just as well as you can see in bright light, though your vision in darkness is in black and white.

special.name: Clan Dagger
special.description: You get one clan dagger for free, as it was given to you at birth. Selling this clan dagger is a terrible taboo and earns you the disdain of other dwarves.

dwarf.description: Dwarves are a short, stocky people who are often stubborn, fierce, and devoted. They have a well-earned reputation as a stoic and stern people, but also have an unbridled zeal and deeply value artisanship. While trust from a dwarf is hard-won, once gained it is as strong as iron.

heritages.name: Ancient-Blooded Dwarf
heritages.description: Trigger: You fail a saving throw against a magical effect. Roll a DC 17 flat check. On a success, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to that saving throw.

heritages.name: Death Warden Dwarf
heritages.description: You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saves against negative energy effects and effects that would give you the drained condition.

heritages.name: Forge Dwarf
heritages.description: You gain fire resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1), and treat environmental heat effects as one step less severe.

heritages.name: Rock Dwarf
heritages.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Fortitude or Reflex DC against Shove or Trip, and a +2 bonus to Reflex saves against falling traps.

heritages.name: Strong-Blooded Dwarf
heritages.description: You gain poison resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1). Each successful save against a poison reduces its stage by 2, or 1 for virulent poison.

heritages.name: Oathkeeper Dwarf
heritages.description: You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Will saves against effects that would make you act against your promises or oaths.

feats.name: Dwarven Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Crafting and Religion. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Dwarven Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You are trained with the battle axe, pick, and warhammer, and all dwarf weapons. Martial dwarf weapons are simple and advanced dwarf weapons are martial for you.

feats.name: Rock Runner
feats.description: You ignore difficult terrain from rubble and uneven ground made of stone or earth.

feats.name: Stonecunning
feats.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception to notice unusual stonework, even without Searching for it.

feats.name: Unburdened Iron
feats.description: You ignore the Speed penalty from armor. When encumbered you can Stride at full Speed but take a -1 circumstance penalty to attack rolls.

feats.name: Vengeful Hatred
feats.description: Choose one: drow, duergars, giants, or orcs. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to damage rolls against that creature type.

feats.name: Boulder Roll
feats.description: You can Shove an enemy up to one size larger than you. On a success, you move them into an adjacent space rather than pushing them away.

feats.name: Dwarven Weapon Cunning
feats.description: Whenever you critically hit using a battle axe, pick, warhammer, or dwarf weapon, apply the weapon critical specialization effect.

feats.name: Stonewalker
feats.description: You gain meld into stone as an innate primal spell once per day. You can sense secret doors in stone surfaces within 5 feet.

elf.name: Elf

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

elf.description: Elves are a tall, long-lived people with a strong tradition of art and magic. As an ancient people, elves have seen great change and have the perspective that only comes from watching the arc of history. They value kindness, intellect, and beauty.

heritages.name: Arctic Elf
heritages.description: You gain cold resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1), and treat environmental cold effects as one step less extreme.

heritages.name: Cavern Elf
heritages.description: You were born or spent many years in underground tunnels where light is scarce. You gain darkvision.

heritages.name: Desert Elf
heritages.description: You gain fire resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1), and treat environmental heat effects as one step less extreme.

heritages.name: Seer Elf
heritages.description: You can cast detect magic as an innate arcane cantrip at will. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Identify Magic and Decipher Writing of a magical nature.

heritages.name: Whisper Elf
heritages.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception when Searching or Seeking hidden creatures. Your flat check for a concealed creature is DC 3 (not 5), and DC 9 for hidden (not 11).

heritages.name: Woodland Elf
heritages.description: When Climbing foliage, you move at half Speed on a success and full Speed on a critical success. Acrobatics successes to Balance on plant matter become critical successes.

feats.name: Ancestral Longevity
feats.description: During daily preparations, gain trained proficiency in one skill of your choice until you next prepare.

feats.name: Elven Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Arcana and Society. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Elven Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You are trained with longbows, composite longbows, longswords, rapiers, shortbows, composite shortbows, and elven curve blades.

feats.name: Forlorn
feats.description: Having lived outside elven society, your proficiency rank for Will saves increases to expert.

feats.name: Nimble Elf
feats.description: Your Speed increases by 5 feet.

feats.name: Otherworldly Magic
feats.description: You can cast one cantrip from the arcane spell list as an innate arcane cantrip at will.

feats.name: Unwavering Mien
feats.description: When you succeed on a save against frightened or confused, you get a critical success instead.

feats.name: Elven Weapon Elegance
feats.description: When you critically hit with a longbow, composite longbow, longsword, rapier, shortbow, composite shortbow, or elven curve blade, apply the critical specialization effect.

feats.name: Resistance to Ruin
feats.description: You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saves against necromancy effects and the doomed condition.

feats.name: Elven Weapon Expertise
feats.description: Your proficiency ranks for longbows, composite longbows, longswords, rapiers, shortbows, composite shortbows, and elven curve blades increase to expert.

gnome.name: Gnome

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

gnome.description: Gnomes are short and hardy folk with an unquenchable curiosity and eccentric habits. Always hungry for new experiences, gnomes wander to stave off the Bleaching, a terrible ailment that drains color from gnomes who fail to dream, innovate, and take in new experiences.

heritages.name: Chameleon Gnome
heritages.description: You can shift the color of your hair, skin, and eyes. You can cast color spray as a 1st-level innate primal spell once per day.

heritages.name: Fey-Touched Gnome
heritages.description: Your connection to the First World is strong. You can cast a cantrip from the primal spell list as an innate primal spell at will.

heritages.name: Sensate Gnome
heritages.description: You gain imprecise scent with a range of 30 feet and a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception against creatures within that range.

heritages.name: Umbral Gnome
heritages.description: You gain darkvision.

heritages.name: Wellspring Gnome
heritages.description: Choose arcane, divine, or occult. You can cast a cantrip from that tradition as an innate spell at will.

feats.name: Animal Accomplice
feats.description: You gain the service of a Tiny animal that does not require Nature expertise. It has one support benefit.

feats.name: Fey Fellowship
feats.description: Your attitude toward fey starts one step more favorable, and you can speak with fey without sharing a language.

feats.name: First World Magic
feats.description: You learn one cantrip from the primal spell list you can cast as an innate primal spell at will.

feats.name: Gnome Obsession
feats.description: You gain trained in a Lore skill of your choice, and can temporarily swap it during daily preparations.

feats.name: Gnome Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You are trained with the glaive, kukri, and all gnome weapons. Martial gnome weapons are simple and advanced gnome weapons are martial for you.

feats.name: Illusion Sense
feats.description: You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saves against illusion effects and to Perception checks to disbelieve illusions.

feats.name: Gnome Weapon Innovator
feats.description: Whenever you critically hit using a gnome weapon, apply the critical specialization effect.

feats.name: Life-Giving Magic
feats.description: When you cast an innate spell of 1st rank or higher that is not a cantrip, you gain temporary HP equal to the spell rank.

feats.name: Energized Font
feats.description: When you cast an innate focus spell, you regain 1 Focus Point up to your maximum.

goblin.name: Goblin

senses.name: Darkvision
senses.description: You can see in darkness and dim light just as well as you can see in bright light, though your vision in darkness is in black and white.

goblin.description: Goblins are a short, scrappy, energetic people who have spent millennia maligned and feared. These small folk live in the moment and prefer tall tales over factual records. Goblin virtues are about being present, creative, and honest.

heritages.name: Charhide Goblin
heritages.description: You gain fire resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1). When you take fire damage, make a DC 5 flat check to avoid gaining persistent fire damage.

heritages.name: Irongut Goblin
heritages.description: You can subsist on non-edible things and gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saves against afflictions and effects that would make you sickened.

heritages.name: Razortooth Goblin
heritages.description: You gain a jaws unarmed attack dealing 1d6 piercing damage with the finesse and unarmed traits.

heritages.name: Snow Goblin
heritages.description: You gain cold resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1), and treat environmental cold effects as one step less severe.

heritages.name: Tailed Goblin
heritages.description: You do not need an adjacent wall to balance on narrow surfaces and gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Athletics checks to Climb.

heritages.name: Treedweller Goblin
heritages.description: You gain a climb Speed of 10 feet and ignore difficult terrain from undergrowth.

feats.name: Burn It!
feats.description: Your spells and alchemical items dealing fire damage gain a status bonus to damage equal to half the spell rank (minimum 1), plus 1 additional persistent fire damage.

feats.name: City Scavenger
feats.description: When you Subsist in a settlement, you can also Earn Income as if using Subsistence without needing Society proficiency.

feats.name: Goblin Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Nature and Stealth. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Goblin Scuttle
feats.description: Trigger: An ally ends its turn adjacent to you. Effect: You Step as a reaction.

feats.name: Goblin Song
feats.description: Attempt a Performance check against one target within 30 feet; on a success, the target is flat-footed until the start of your next turn.

feats.name: Goblin Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You are trained with the dogslicer, horsechopper, and all goblin weapons. Martial goblin weapons are simple and advanced goblin weapons are martial for you.

feats.name: Junk Tinker
feats.description: You can Craft using junk or discarded materials, reducing the cost by 25%.

feats.name: Goblin Weapon Frenzy
feats.description: Whenever you critically hit using a goblin weapon, apply the critical specialization effect.

feats.name: Roll with It
feats.description: Trigger: A creature succeeds on a melee Strike against you. You roll with the blow, taking half damage and being pushed 10 feet, landing prone.

feats.name: Very Sneaky
feats.description: You can Hide and Sneak even while observed. When undetected, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Stealth and initiative.

halfling.name: Halfling

senses.name: Keen Eyes
senses.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Seek hidden or undetected creatures within 30 feet. The flat check to target a concealed creature is DC 3 (not 5), and DC 9 for hidden (not 11).

halfling.description: Halflings are a short, adaptable people who exhibit remarkable curiosity, pluck, and a love of creature comforts. Easily assumed to be harmless, their dexterous fingers and nimble minds make them surprisingly capable.

heritages.name: Gutsy Halfling
heritages.description: You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saves against emotion effects. When you succeed on a save against an emotion effect, you get a critical success instead.

heritages.name: Hillock Halfling
heritages.description: Whenever you rest and recover, you regain additional HP equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum 0).

heritages.name: Jinxed Halfling
heritages.description: You can cast ill omen as an innate occult cantrip at will.

heritages.name: Nomadic Halfling
heritages.description: Your ancestors traveled from place to place. You gain two additional languages of your choice.

heritages.name: Twilight Halfling
heritages.description: Your ancestors made homes in dim forests. You gain low-light vision.

heritages.name: Wildwood Halfling
heritages.description: You are not slowed by difficult terrain from undergrowth and forest environments.

feats.name: Distracting Shadows
feats.description: You can use creatures at least one size larger than you as cover to Hide and Sneak.

feats.name: Halfling Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Acrobatics and Stealth. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Halfling Luck
feats.description: Trigger: You fail a skill check or saving throw. You can reroll it but must use the second result even if worse.

feats.name: Halfling Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You are trained with slings, halfling sling staves, and shortswords, and with all halfling weapons.

feats.name: Innocuous
feats.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Deception checks to pass as a non-threat.

feats.name: Titan Slinger
feats.description: When using a sling, increase the damage die by one step against Large or larger creatures.

feats.name: Halfling Weapon Trickster
feats.description: You apply critical specialization effects with shortswords and halfling weapons.

feats.name: Vivacious Conduit
feats.description: When healed with a heal spell, you recover additional HP equal to your Constitution modifier.

feats.name: Extreme Luck
feats.description: When you use Halfling Luck and still fail, you can choose not to use the result and treat it as a failure.

human.name: Human

bonus_pool: Any common language or language prevalent in your region

human.description: Humans are incredibly diverse. Some are decisive warriors who win glory on the battlefield, while others are careful diplomats who prefer the pen to the sword. Humans are exceptionally adaptable, thriving in nearly any environment and culture.

heritages.name: Skilled Heritage
heritages.description: You become trained in one skill of your choice. At 5th level, you become an expert in that skill.

heritages.name: Versatile Heritage
heritages.description: Humanity adaptability manifests as a general feat of your choice for which you meet the prerequisites.

heritages.name: Half-Elf
heritages.description: You have human and elven ancestry. You gain low-light vision and access to elf and half-elf ancestry feats in addition to human ancestry feats.

heritages.name: Half-Orc
heritages.description: You have human and orcish ancestry. You gain low-light vision or darkvision if you already have it. You can select orc and half-orc ancestry feats in addition to human feats.

heritages.name: Wintertouched Human
heritages.description: You gain cold resistance 5 and treat environmental cold effects as one step less severe.

heritages.name: Wandering Human
heritages.description: Your line has traveled across many lands. You gain additional languages equal to half your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1).

feats.name: Adapted Cantrip
feats.description: Choose one cantrip from a magical tradition other than your own. You can cast it as an innate spell of that tradition at will.

feats.name: Cooperative Nature
feats.description: You gain a +4 circumstance bonus on checks to Aid another creature.

feats.name: General Training
feats.description: Your varied background grants you a general feat for which you meet the prerequisites.

feats.name: Haughty Obstinacy
feats.description: If you roll a success on a save against a mental effect that would give the controlled condition, you get a critical success instead.

feats.name: Natural Ambition
feats.description: You gain a 1st-level class feat for which you meet the prerequisites.

feats.name: Natural Skill
feats.description: You gain trained proficiency in two additional skills of your choice.

feats.name: Unconventional Weaponry
feats.description: Choose one uncommon weapon. You gain trained proficiency in it.

feats.name: Adaptive Adept
feats.description: Choose one spell of 1st rank or higher from the same tradition as your Adapted Cantrip. You can cast it as an innate spell once per day.

feats.name: Clever Improviser
feats.description: You can attempt skill checks for skills you are untrained in without the usual penalty.

feats.name: Multitalented
feats.description: You gain a 2nd-level multiclass dedication feat even without taking its prerequisite delegation, as long as you meet all other prerequisites.

leshy.name: Leshy

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

special.name: Plant Nourishment
special.description: You gain nourishment through photosynthesis, absorbing minerals, or scavenging decaying matter. You typically do not need to pay for food. If you rely on photosynthesis and go without sunlight for 1 week, you begin to starve.

leshy.description: Leshies are small plant-like humanoids created and nurtured by druids. Each leshy is unique, but all share an affinity for nature and a curiosity about the world. They exist in a wondrous state of innocent wonder, fascinated by everything they encounter.

heritages.name: Cactus Leshy
heritages.description: You gain a spine unarmed attack dealing 1d6 piercing damage. When a creature damages you with a non-reach melee attack, it takes 1 piercing damage.

heritages.name: Fruit Leshy
heritages.description: Once per day, a creature can eat your fruit as a single action to recover 1d8 HP. The fruit regrows in 24 hours.

heritages.name: Fungus Leshy
heritages.description: You are a mushroom leshy. You gain darkvision and are immune to the sickened condition from ingested sources.

heritages.name: Gourd Leshy
heritages.description: You have a hollow gourd for a head that can store one Bulk 0 item inside.

heritages.name: Leaf Leshy
heritages.description: Your leaves slow your descent. You take no damage from falling and can Glide 5 feet forward per 10 feet of descent.

heritages.name: Vine Leshy
heritages.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Athletics checks to Grapple and gain the Improved Grab action.

feats.name: Leshy Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Nature and Stealth. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Grasping Reach
feats.description: Your melee reach increases by 5 feet for the purpose of making Grapple checks and interacting with objects.

feats.name: Seedpod
feats.description: You gain a seedpod ranged unarmed attack with a 30-foot range dealing 1d6 bludgeoning damage with the agile, finesse, and unarmed traits.

feats.name: Leshy Familiar
feats.description: You gain a familiar with the plant trait with two abilities of your choice.

feats.name: Call of the Wild
feats.description: You can cast animal messenger as an innate primal spell once per day.

feats.name: Ritual Reversion
feats.description: Once per day, you can transform into a Small plant. While transformed, you are stationary, gain regeneration 5, and appear as a mundane plant.

orc.name: Orc

senses.name: Darkvision
senses.description: You can see in darkness and dim light just as well as you can see in bright light, though your vision in darkness is in black and white.

orc.description: Orcs are a proud, strong people with a rich warrior culture and deep spiritual traditions. Modern orcs struggle against a violent reputation that many are working to change, and are increasingly found integrated into other societies.

heritages.name: Badlands Orc
heritages.description: You gain fire resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1), and treat environmental heat effects as one step less severe.

heritages.name: Battle-Ready Orc
heritages.description: Your proficiency rank for simple weapons and unarmed attacks increases to expert.

heritages.name: Deep Orc
heritages.description: Your life among subterranean passages grants you Terrain Expertise for underground terrain.

heritages.name: Grave Orc
heritages.description: You gain negative resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1).

heritages.name: Hold-Scarred Orc
heritages.description: You gain resistance to mental damage equal to half your level (minimum 1).

heritages.name: Rainfall Orc
heritages.description: You have adapted to wet environments. You gain a swim Speed of 15 feet.

feats.name: Bloody Blows
feats.description: Once per round when you hit a creature and deal damage, that creature takes 1 persistent bleed damage.

feats.name: Orc Ferocity
feats.description: Trigger: You would be reduced to 0 HP. You avoid being knocked out and remain at 1 HP instead. Once per day.

feats.name: Orc Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Athletics and Survival. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Orc Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You are trained with falchions, greataxes, and all orc weapons. Martial orc weapons are simple and advanced orc weapons are martial for you.

feats.name: Hold Mark
feats.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Intimidation checks to Demoralize creatures from cultures that recognize your orc hold marking.

feats.name: Orc Weapon Carnage
feats.description: Whenever you critically hit using a falchion, greataxe, or orc weapon, apply the critical specialization effect.

feats.name: Victorious Vigor
feats.description: When you knock a foe out or kill them, you regain 1d8 temporary HP plus 1d8 per 4 levels.

feats.name: Incredible Ferocity
feats.description: Your Orc Ferocity triggers once per encounter rather than once per day.

catfolk.name: Catfolk

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

catfolk.description: Catfolk are highly social, feline humanoids prone to curiosity and wandering. They combine the features of felines and humanoids in both appearance and temperament, enjoying learning new things and collecting trinkets. They view themselves as the chosen guardians of natural places.

heritages.name: Clawed Catfolk
heritages.description: You gain a claw unarmed attack dealing 1d6 slashing damage with the agile, finesse, and unarmed traits.

heritages.name: Hunting Catfolk
heritages.description: You gain the Terrain Stalker feat even without meeting its prerequisites.

heritages.name: Jungle Catfolk
heritages.description: You gain a climb Speed of 10 feet and a swim Speed of 10 feet.

heritages.name: Nine Lives Catfolk
heritages.description: Once per day, when damage would reduce you to 0 HP, make a DC 16 flat check to remain at 1 HP instead.

heritages.name: Sharp-Eared Catfolk
heritages.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks involving hearing.

heritages.name: Tailed Catfolk
heritages.description: You have a prehensile tail that can Interact with objects. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Acrobatics checks to Balance.

feats.name: Cat Nap
feats.description: Once per hour, you can spend 10 minutes resting to recover 1d6 HP per level.

feats.name: Catfolk Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Acrobatics and Survival. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Catfolk Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You are trained with kukris, sickles, and all catfolk weapons.

feats.name: Land on Your Feet
feats.description: When you fall, you take only half the normal damage and do not land prone.

feats.name: Catfolk Weapon Raking
feats.description: Whenever you critically hit using a catfolk weapon, apply the critical specialization effect.

feats.name: Evade Doom
feats.description: Once per round when you roll a success on a Reflex save, you treat the result as a critical success.

feats.name: Eerie Flexibility
feats.description: You can Squeeze through tight spaces as though they were normal terrain.

fetchling.name: Fetchling

senses.name: Darkvision
senses.description: You can see in darkness and dim light just as well as you can see in bright light, though your vision in darkness is in black and white.

fetchling.description: Fetchlings are a people shaped by the Shadow Plane, descended from humans who became stranded there long ago. They walk between shadow and light, comfortable in darkness as in daylight. Many serve as scouts and diplomats between planes.

heritages.name: Liminal Fetchling
heritages.description: You exist at the boundary between light and shadow. You can cast dim the light as an innate occult cantrip at will.

heritages.name: Noisome Fetchling
heritages.description: Creatures adjacent to you take a -1 circumstance penalty to Perception checks.

heritages.name: Resolute Fetchling
heritages.description: Hardship in the Shadow Plane has made you resilient. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saves against mental effects.

heritages.name: Shade Fetchling
heritages.description: You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to spell attack rolls and spell DCs when casting spells with the shadow trait.

heritages.name: Tenebrism Fetchling
heritages.description: Once per turn, when in dim light or darkness, you can teleport 5 feet to another location in dim light or darkness as a free action.

feats.name: Fetchling Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Stealth and Occultism. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Shadow Blending
feats.description: In areas of dim light, you are concealed rather than needing to use Stealth.

feats.name: Subtle Shadow
feats.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Stealth checks when hiding in dim light or darkness.

feats.name: Shadow Pockets
feats.description: You can produce small items from shadow as a free action, even without a physical pocket.

feats.name: Shadowy Teleportation
feats.description: Once per day, you can cast shadow walk as an innate occult spell.

hobgoblin.name: Hobgoblin

senses.name: Darkvision
senses.description: You can see in darkness and dim light just as well as you can see in bright light, though your vision in darkness is in black and white.

hobgoblin.description: Hobgoblins are a militaristic people who excel at warfare and discipline. They have built powerful nations and earned a reputation as merciless warriors, but modern hobgoblins are just as often found as valued members of adventuring parties and diverse societies.

heritages.name: Elfbane Hobgoblin
heritages.description: You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saves against arcane magical effects.

heritages.name: Runtboss Hobgoblin
heritages.description: You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Intimidation checks and can Demoralize as a single action.

heritages.name: Smokeworker Hobgoblin
heritages.description: You gain fire resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1) and do not treat smoke as difficult terrain.

heritages.name: Totembound Hobgoblin
heritages.description: Choose a totem animal. You gain low-light vision and a +1 circumstance bonus to Nature checks relating to your totem animal.

heritages.name: Warmarch Hobgoblin
heritages.description: You can Hustle during overland travel for twice as long before becoming fatigued.

feats.name: Alchemical Scholar
feats.description: You are trained in Crafting and gain the Alchemical Crafting feat.

feats.name: Hobgoblin Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Athletics and Intimidation. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Hobgoblin Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You are trained with composite shortbows, composite longbows, longswords, and all goblin weapons.

feats.name: Leech-Clipper
feats.description: You do not take a penalty for attacking a creature adjacent to you or an ally when they are grappled together.

feats.name: Hobgoblin Weapon Discipline
feats.description: Whenever you critically hit using a goblin weapon, composite shortbow, composite longbow, or longsword, apply the critical specialization effect.

feats.name: Pride in Arms
feats.description: When you succeed at a Strike, you can grant a +1 circumstance bonus to the next attack roll an ally makes against the same target before the start of your next turn.

feats.name: Formation Attack
feats.description: When adjacent to an ally who is also adjacent to your target, your Strikes deal an extra 1d6 damage.

kholo.name: Kholo

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

kholo.description: Kholo (also called gnolls) are powerfully-built humanoids resembling hyenas. They are cunning hunters who live in clans led by powerful matriarchs, prizing strength and cunning. Their society is built around the hunt and the bond of the clan.

heritages.name: Sweetbreath Kholo
heritages.description: Once per day, you can exhale a cloud in a 10-foot emanation. Creatures must succeed at a Fortitude save against your class DC or become confused for 1 round.

heritages.name: Witch-Tooth Kholo
heritages.description: Your jaws unarmed attack deals 1d8 piercing damage. On a critical hit, the target takes 1d6 persistent bleed damage.

heritages.name: Cunning Kholo
heritages.description: You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to skill checks involving tracking, hunting, or pursuit.

heritages.name: Packmate Kholo
heritages.description: When adjacent to at least one ally, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to attack rolls.

feats.name: Bone Gnaw
feats.description: You have a jaws unarmed attack dealing 1d6 piercing damage. On a critical hit, the target is also clumsy 1 until the start of your next turn.

feats.name: Kholo Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Intimidation and Survival. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Kholo Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You are trained with falchions, flails, and all kholo weapons.

feats.name: Pack Tactics
feats.description: When you flank a creature with an ally, you deal an extra 1d4 damage with your Strikes.

feats.name: Kholo Weapon Expertise
feats.description: Whenever you critically hit using a kholo weapon, falchion, or flail, apply the critical specialization effect.

kitsune.name: Kitsune

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

kitsune.description: Kitsune are a charismatic and witty people with a connection to the spiritual that grants them a supernatural talent for illusion and transformation. Known as excellent tricksters and diplomats, they can shift form to interact with others more easily.

heritages.name: Dark Fields Kitsune
heritages.description: You gain darkvision and can cast darkness as an innate occult spell once per day.

heritages.name: Earthly Wilds Kitsune
heritages.description: You can cast speak with animals as an innate primal spell once per day.

heritages.name: Empty Sky Kitsune
heritages.description: You gain a fly Speed of 10 feet for 1 minute once per day.

heritages.name: Iron Peninsula Kitsune
heritages.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saves against mental effects.

heritages.name: Lunar Kitsune
heritages.description: You can cast moon frenzy as an innate primal spell once per day.

heritages.name: Star Kitsune
heritages.description: You can cast dancing lights as an innate occult cantrip at will.

feats.name: Kitsune Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Deception and Occultism. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Kitsune Spell Familiarity
feats.description: You can cast illusory disguise as an innate occult spell once per day.

feats.name: Fox Trick
feats.description: Once per day, you can cast cloak of colors as an innate occult spell.

feats.name: Meddlesome Fingers
feats.description: In your fox form, you can use simple tools and perform Interact actions as if you had hands.

feats.name: Many Tails
feats.description: You manifest an additional tail. Each tail grants one additional use of your innate ancestry spell per day.

feats.name: Inari's Guidance
feats.description: Once per day, you can cast augury as an innate occult spell.

feats.name: Fox Shape
feats.description: You can cast pest form at will as an innate primal spell, but only into a fox form.

kobold.name: Kobold

senses.name: Darkvision
senses.description: You can see in darkness and dim light just as well as you can see in bright light, though your vision in darkness is in black and white.

kobold.description: Kobolds are small, reptilian humanoids with a proud draconic heritage. They are cunning survivalists who excel at trap-making and tunnel fighting. Though often looked down upon by larger peoples, kobolds have fierce pride and a tight-knit sense of community.

heritages.name: Cavern Kobold
heritages.description: You are adapted to underground environments. You gain a climb Speed of 10 feet.

heritages.name: Dragonscaled Kobold
heritages.description: You gain resistance to one energy type based on your draconic color (acid, cold, electricity, fire, or poison) equal to half your level (minimum 1).

heritages.name: Spellscale Kobold
heritages.description: You can cast one cantrip from the arcane spell list as an innate arcane cantrip at will.

heritages.name: Strong-Jaw Kobold
heritages.description: Your jaws are particularly powerful. You gain a jaws unarmed attack dealing 1d6 piercing damage.

heritages.name: Venomtail Kobold
heritages.description: You gain a tail Strike unarmed attack dealing 1d4 poison damage with the agile, finesse, and unarmed traits.

feats.name: Cringe
feats.description: When a creature attacks you, you can cringe as a reaction. The creature must succeed on a Will save or become off-guard against all attacks until the start of its next turn.

feats.name: Kobold Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Acrobatics and Crafting. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Kobold Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You are trained with picks, spears, and all kobold weapons.

feats.name: Snare Setter
feats.description: You gain the Snare Crafting skill feat and can Craft snares in half the normal time.

feats.name: Kobold Weapon Specialist
feats.description: Whenever you critically hit using a kobold weapon, pick, or spear, apply the critical specialization effect.

feats.name: Winglets
feats.description: Small vestigial wings emerge from your back. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Acrobatics and can Glide 5 feet forward per 10 feet of descent.

lizardfolk.name: Lizardfolk

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

special.name: Claws
special.description: You have a claw unarmed attack that deals 1d4 slashing damage with the agile and unarmed traits.

lizardfolk.description: Lizardfolk are proud, strong people who live in tribal communities in warm and tropical regions. Known as Iruxi among their own kind, they have scaled skin and natural athleticism. They tend to be practical and direct, valuing action over deliberation.

heritages.name: Cliffscale Lizardfolk
heritages.description: You can cling to surfaces with your toes. You gain a climb Speed of 20 feet.

heritages.name: Frilled Lizardfolk
heritages.description: Once per day, you can fan your frill to frighten enemies. All enemies in a 20-foot emanation must attempt a Will save against your class DC or become frightened 1.

heritages.name: Sandstrider Lizardfolk
heritages.description: You gain fire resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1) and treat environmental heat as one step less severe.

heritages.name: Unseen Lizardfolk
heritages.description: You can rapidly shift your scale pigmentation. You can cast blur as an innate primal spell once per day.

heritages.name: Wetlander Lizardfolk
heritages.description: You excel in aquatic environments. You gain a swim Speed of 25 feet.

heritages.name: Woodstalker Lizardfolk
heritages.description: You can Hide in forests and undergrowth even without cover.

feats.name: Iruxi Unarmed Cunning
feats.description: Your claw unarmed attack deals 1d6 slashing damage instead of 1d4.

feats.name: Lizardfolk Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Athletics and Survival. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Marsh Runner
feats.description: You move through swamps and marshes at full Speed and treat mud and shallow water as normal terrain.

feats.name: Parthenogenic Hatchling
feats.description: You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saves against effects that would cause you to act against your will.

feats.name: Iruxi Unarmed Expertise
feats.description: Your proficiency with your claw unarmed attack increases to expert.

feats.name: Tongue Pull
feats.description: You gain a tongue ranged unarmed attack with a range of 10 feet that can Grapple or disarm a creature.

feats.name: Tail Whip
feats.description: You gain a tail unarmed attack dealing 1d6 bludgeoning damage with the trip trait.

ratfolk.name: Ratfolk

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

special.name: Sharp Teeth
special.description: You have a jaws unarmed attack that deals 1d4 piercing damage, is in the brawling group, and has the agile and finesse traits.

ratfolk.description: Ratfolk are quick, clever people who thrive in diverse communities and dynamic environments. Known for curiosity and skill with crafting and trade, they form tight bonds with their family groups. Despite their reputation as scrappy survivors, ratfolk often excel as merchants, scholars, and adventurers.

heritages.name: Deep Rat
heritages.description: You come from underground warrens. You gain darkvision.

heritages.name: Desert Rat
heritages.description: You gain fire resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1) and treat environmental heat as one step less severe.

heritages.name: Longsnout Rat
heritages.description: Your long snout gives you a powerful sense of smell. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks that rely on smell.

heritages.name: Sewer Rat
heritages.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saves against diseases and poisons.

heritages.name: Shadow Rat
heritages.description: You can Hide in areas of dim light without cover.

heritages.name: Snow Rat
heritages.description: You gain cold resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1) and treat environmental cold as one step less severe.

feats.name: Cheek Pouches
feats.description: Your cheeks can expand remarkably. You can store items with a total Bulk of 1 in your cheeks.

feats.name: Ratfolk Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Acrobatics and Stealth. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Ratfolk Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You are trained with the shortsword, sickle, and all ratfolk weapons.

feats.name: Sturdy
feats.description: Your compact frame is surprisingly resilient. Your maximum Hit Points increase by 2.

feats.name: Ratfolk Weapon Specialist
feats.description: Whenever you critically hit using a ratfolk weapon, shortsword, or sickle, apply the critical specialization effect.

feats.name: Vicious Incisors
feats.description: Your incisors are formidable. You gain a jaws unarmed attack dealing 1d6 piercing damage.

feats.name: Overcrowd
feats.description: You can move through spaces occupied by allies without needing to Tumble Through.

tengu.name: Tengu

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

tengu.description: Tengus are avian humanoids who collect memories, traditions, and skills from the cultures they encounter. They are talented mimics with a sharp eye for detail and an even sharper tongue. Tengus excel as fighters and scholars, mastering skills from many cultural traditions.

heritages.name: Dogtooth Tengu
heritages.description: Your beak deals 1d8 piercing damage, and on a critical hit the target takes 1d4 persistent bleed damage.

heritages.name: Jinxed Tengu
heritages.description: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an unarmed attack, you can give it a -1 circumstance penalty to its next saving throw.

heritages.name: Mountainkeeper Tengu
heritages.description: You have practiced spiritual traditions. You gain trained in Religion or Occultism (your choice).

heritages.name: Skyborn Tengu
heritages.description: Your lightweight bones allow you to Leap prodigiously. When you Leap, treat it as having a fly speed of 10 feet for determining distance.

heritages.name: Stormtossed Tengu
heritages.description: You gain electricity resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1).

heritages.name: Taloned Tengu
heritages.description: You gain a talon unarmed attack dealing 1d4 slashing damage with agile and finesse traits.

feats.name: Eat Fortune
feats.description: Once per day, when a creature uses a fortune effect on you, you can negate it as a reaction.

feats.name: Squawk!
feats.description: Once per round, you can Demoralize a creature as a free action using Intimidation.

feats.name: Tengu Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Acrobatics and Intimidation. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Tengu Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You are trained with katanas, khakkharas, naginatas, wakizashis, and all tengu weapons.

feats.name: Tengu Weapon Expertise
feats.description: Whenever you critically hit using a tengu weapon, katana, khaqqhara, naginata, or wakizashi, apply the critical specialization effect.

feats.name: Soaring Flight
feats.description: Once per day, you can gain a fly Speed of 20 feet for 10 minutes.

feats.name: Wind-Up Tengu
feats.description: You gain a permanent fly Speed of 20 feet.

tripkee.name: Tripkee

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

special.name: Adhesive Feet
special.description: Your feet can cling to surfaces. You gain a climb Speed of 15 feet.

tripkee.description: Tripkees are small, frog-like humanoids with sticky hands and feet and an innate connection to nature and rain. They build communities in tropical and subtropical rainforests, with culture revolving around water, spirits, and the rhythms of the natural world.

heritages.name: Poisonous Tripkee
heritages.description: Creatures that grapple you or Strike you with an unarmed attack take 1d4 poison damage.

heritages.name: Sticky Tripkee
heritages.description: You gain a +4 circumstance bonus to checks to maintain a grapple and to Climb, and can Climb at full Speed.

heritages.name: Swimmer Tripkee
heritages.description: You gain a swim Speed of 25 feet and can breathe underwater.

heritages.name: Camouflage Tripkee
heritages.description: You can rapidly change your skin color. You can Hide even while observed, and gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Stealth checks.

feats.name: Tongue Strike
feats.description: You gain a tongue unarmed attack with a reach of 10 feet dealing 1d4 bludgeoning damage.

feats.name: Tripkee Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Nature and Stealth. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Rain Dance
feats.description: Once per day, you can cast obscuring mist as an innate primal spell.

feats.name: Leaping Pounce
feats.description: When you Leap, you can make one melee Strike against any creature in the area of your leap as part of the same action.

feats.name: Spirit Caller
feats.description: Once per day, you can cast commune with nature as an innate primal spell.

athamaru.name: Athamaru

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

special.name: Swim Speed
special.description: You have a swim Speed of 25 feet.

special.name: Pheromone Communication
special.description: You can communicate basic emotions chemically to other athamaru within 30 feet without speaking.

athamaru.description: Athamarus are fish-like humanoids who form tight-knit communities underseas, with villages led by a common matriarch. They engage in subsistence farming of seaweed, train eels as mounts, and create elaborate works of coral art. They value community above all else.

heritages.name: Bioluminescent Athamaru
heritages.description: Your body produces light. You can activate or deactivate this as a free action, producing dim light in a 10-foot radius.

heritages.name: Deepwater Athamaru
heritages.description: You are adapted to the crushing pressure of deep water. You can breathe at any depth and ignore environmental effects of deep water pressure.

heritages.name: Eel-Rider Athamaru
heritages.description: You are particularly bonded to eels. You gain the Mount Companion feat for free and your eel companion counts as a trained animal companion.

heritages.name: Reef-Builder Athamaru
heritages.description: Your coral adornments have grown into your body. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your AC.

feats.name: Athamaru Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Athletics and Society. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Pheromone Mastery
feats.description: Your pheromone control is exceptional. You can communicate complex ideas to other athamaru within 60 feet without speaking.

feats.name: Coral Crafting
feats.description: You gain the Craft skill feat and can craft items from living coral, which regrow if properly tended.

feats.name: Eel Mount Mastery
feats.description: Your eel mount is treated as an expert animal companion and gains its support benefit.

feats.name: Matriarch's Resolve
feats.description: Once per day, when you would be reduced to 0 HP, you remain at 1 HP and grow to Large size for 1 minute.

azarketi.name: Azarketi

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

special.name: Hydration
special.description: Your body requires you to submerge in water at least once per 24-hour period. After 24 hours on land, you take a -1 status penalty to Fortitude saves. After 48 hours, you begin to suffocate until returned to water.

special.name: Swim Speed
special.description: You have a swim Speed of 30 feet.

azarketi.description: Azarketis are amphibious humanoids descended from humans who were magically transformed by Alghollthus long ago. They maintain a connection to the sea and have developed their own culture and identity separate from their origins.
azarketi.source: Absalom, City of Lost Omens pg. 393

heritages.name: Ancient Osiriani Azarketi
heritages.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Recall Knowledge checks about ancient history and Azlanti artifacts.

heritages.name: Benthic Azarketi
heritages.description: You come from the deep ocean. You gain darkvision and can breathe in water at any depth without penalty.

heritages.name: Mistborne Azarketi
heritages.description: You were born in mist-shrouded coastal areas. You are concealed in areas of mist or fog even without cover.

heritages.name: Vivacious Azarketi
heritages.description: Your connection to the water is invigorating. When you start your turn in water, you gain 1d4 temporary HP.

feats.name: Azarketi Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Athletics and Arcana. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Hydraulic Deflection
feats.description: Once per round as a reaction, you create a watery barrier giving you a +1 circumstance bonus to AC.

feats.name: Murk Diver
feats.description: You ignore concealment from murky water and gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Stealth in aquatic environments.

feats.name: Beckoning Wave
feats.description: Once per day, you can cast hydraulic push as a 2nd-rank innate primal spell.

feats.name: Tidal Leap
feats.description: When emerging from water, you can Leap horizontally up to your Speed without a check.

centaur.name: Centaur

senses.name: Darkvision
senses.description: You can see in darkness and dim light just as well as you can see in bright light, though your vision in darkness is in black and white.

special.name: Hooves
special.description: You gain a hoof unarmed attack dealing 1d8 bludgeoning damage.

special.name: Quadruped
special.description: Your four legs grant a +2 circumstance bonus to Athletics checks to resist being knocked prone and to saves against effects that would move you.

centaur.description: Centaurs are proud nomads who range far and wide across their ancestral territories, protecting their lands from exploitation and intrusion. They are survivalists who forge tight bonds with family and community and stand firm in the face of danger.

heritages.name: Forest Centaur
heritages.description: You ignore difficult terrain from undergrowth and gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Survival checks in forested areas.

heritages.name: Mountain Centaur
heritages.description: You ignore difficult terrain from rocky ground and gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Athletics checks to Climb.

heritages.name: Plains Centaur
heritages.description: Your Speed increases by 5 feet, and you can Hustle twice as long during overland travel before becoming fatigued.

heritages.name: Stormchaser Centaur
heritages.description: You gain electricity resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1) and ignore difficult terrain from rain and heavy wind.

feats.name: Centaur Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Athletics and Nature. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Charge!
feats.description: When you Stride at least 10 feet and then Strike with your hooves, deal an extra 1d8 damage and attempt to Trip the target as a free action.

feats.name: Centaur Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You are trained with composite longbows, composite shortbows, lances, and all centaur weapons.

feats.name: Trample
feats.description: You can Stride through the space of prone creatures, dealing your hoof damage to each one (basic Reflex save).

feats.name: War Gallop
feats.description: When you use Charge!, you can move up to twice your Speed before Striking.

merfolk.name: Merfolk

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

special.name: Swim Speed
special.description: You have a swim Speed of 25 feet.

special.name: Aquatic Adaptation
special.description: You can breathe both air and water. Your land Speed of 5 feet reflects your body not being built for land travel.

merfolk.description: Merfolk are aquatic humanoids with the upper body of a humanoid and the lower body of a fish. They are graceful swimmers who build civilizations beneath the waves. Though slow on land, merfolk are powerful allies in their natural aquatic environment.

heritages.name: Benthic Merfolk
heritages.description: You come from the deep ocean. You gain darkvision and can breathe at any depth without penalty.

heritages.name: Shallows Merfolk
heritages.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Athletics checks in shallow water or when dealing with tides and currents.

heritages.name: Songcaller Merfolk
heritages.description: Your voice carries through water. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Performance and Diplomacy checks.

heritages.name: Wavewarden Merfolk
heritages.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to attacks and saves against creatures with the aquatic trait.

feats.name: Hydraulic Deflection
feats.description: Once per round as a reaction, you summon a watery barrier that grants you a +1 circumstance bonus to AC.

feats.name: Merfolk Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Athletics and Nature. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Sea Singer
feats.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Performance checks and can cast haunting hymn as an innate occult cantrip at will.

feats.name: Tail Strike
feats.description: You gain a tail Strike unarmed attack dealing 1d6 bludgeoning damage with the finesse and unarmed traits.

feats.name: Water Dance
feats.description: Once per day, you can cast hydraulic torrent as an innate primal spell.

feats.name: Ocean Stride
feats.description: Your land Speed increases to 15 feet as you learn to move on your tail on land.

minotaur.name: Minotaur

senses.name: Darkvision
senses.description: You can see in darkness and dim light just as well as you can see in bright light, though your vision in darkness is in black and white.

special.name: Horns
special.description: You have powerful horns. You gain a horns unarmed attack dealing 1d8 piercing damage with the shove trait.

minotaur.description: Minotaurs are large, powerful humanoids with the head and horns of a bull. They have a legendary sense of direction and rarely get lost. Modern minotaurs have built their own cultures and often struggle against the violent reputation imposed by stories of their ancestors.

heritages.name: Dungeon Minotaur
heritages.description: You have lived in labyrinthine underground warrens. You gain darkvision and a +2 circumstance bonus to Survival checks to navigate dungeons and underground environments.

heritages.name: Island Minotaur
heritages.description: Your people made their home on a remote island. You gain a swim Speed of 20 feet and a +2 circumstance bonus to Athletics checks to swim.

heritages.name: Mountain Minotaur
heritages.description: You are adapted to high altitudes. You ignore difficult terrain from rocky or mountainous environments.

heritages.name: Maze-Walker Minotaur
heritages.description: Your legendary sense of direction is even stronger. You can never become lost and always know which direction is north.

feats.name: Labyrinthine Recall
feats.description: You can always retrace any path you have taken, even in a maze, without a check.

feats.name: Minotaur Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Athletics and Survival. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Powerful Charge
feats.description: When you Stride at least 10 feet and then use your horns Strike, you deal an extra 1d6 damage.

feats.name: Roar
feats.description: Once per encounter, you can let out a thunderous roar. All enemies within 30 feet attempt a Will save against your class DC or become frightened 1 (frightened 2 on a critical failure).

feats.name: Gore and Trample
feats.description: When you use Powerful Charge, you can attempt to Shove the target. On a success, you can Stride into the target previous space.

nagaji.name: Nagaji

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

special.name: Scaled Skin
special.description: Your scales provide you with a +1 circumstance bonus to your AC against physical damage.

nagaji.description: Nagaji are a serpentine race created to serve the naga. They are strong, scaled humanoids with serpentine features and a proud warrior culture. Modern nagaji have broken free of their creators and built their own societies throughout eastern Golarion.

heritages.name: Clinging Nagaji
heritages.description: Your scales can cling to surfaces. You gain a climb Speed of 20 feet.

heritages.name: Venomous Nagaji
heritages.description: You have potent venom in your bite. You gain a fang unarmed attack dealing 1d6 piercing plus 1d4 poison damage.

heritages.name: Skilled Nagaji
heritages.description: Your heritage focused on intellectual capabilities. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to checks to Recall Knowledge.

heritages.name: Stalker Nagaji
heritages.description: You are trained in ambush tactics. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Stealth checks and do not need to reduce your Speed to Sneak.

feats.name: Nagaji Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Athletics and Intimidation. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Resistant Scales
feats.description: You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saves against poison effects.

feats.name: Serpent's Tongue
feats.description: You gain the scent sense (imprecise, 30 feet) and a +1 circumstance bonus to Perception checks that rely on smell.

feats.name: Naga's Gaze
feats.description: Once per day, you can cast charm as a 2nd-rank innate occult spell.

feats.name: Superior Venom
feats.description: Your venom is exceptionally potent. You can envenom weapons as a free action, and your natural venom deals 2d4 persistent poison damage.

samsaran.name: Samsaran

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

special.name: Lifebound
special.description: Once per day, you can attempt a Recall Knowledge check using any skill, even if you are untrained, representing dim memories from a previous incarnation.

samsaran.description: Samsarans are blue-skinned people who reincarnate upon death and recall pieces of their past lives. They are thoughtful and philosophical, eternally cycling through life and death, each samsaran an ancient soul in a new body accumulating wisdom across incarnations.

heritages.name: Shards of the Past Samsaran
heritages.description: Your past-life memories are unusually vivid. You gain trained in two skills of your choice representing knowledge from a past life.

heritages.name: Mystic Past Life Samsaran
heritages.description: You recall magical knowledge from a past life. You can cast one cantrip from any tradition as an innate spell at will.

heritages.name: Peaceful Samsaran
heritages.description: Your cycle of rebirth has made you serene. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Will saves and are immune to the frightened condition.

feats.name: Samsaran Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Occultism and Religion. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Past-Life Knowledge
feats.description: Once per day, you can use any skill as though you were trained in it.

feats.name: Eerie Resemblance
feats.description: Once per day, you can cast read omens as an innate occult spell relating to one creature you can see.

feats.name: Reincarnated Resolve
feats.description: You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saves against death effects and when recovering from dying.

feats.name: Recall Life
feats.description: Once per day, if you would die, attempt a DC 20 flat check. On a success, you return to life with 1 HP after 1 round.

tanuki.name: Tanuki

special.name: Magical Belly
special.description: Your belly carries innate magic. Once per day, you can cast a 1st-rank illusion spell of your choice as an innate primal spell.

tanuki.description: Tanukis are jolly, raccoon-like humanoids from eastern Golarion who love food, drink, and good company. Known for their magical shapeshifting abilities and association with fortune, tanukis are natural merchants and entertainers who bring good cheer wherever they go.

heritages.name: Feast Tanuki
heritages.description: Your magical belly can produce food and drink. Once per day, you can produce enough food and drink to sustain four Medium creatures for one day.

heritages.name: Forest Tanuki
heritages.description: You are comfortable in woodland environments. You ignore difficult terrain in forested areas and gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Survival checks in forests.

heritages.name: Shapechanger Tanuki
heritages.description: Your shapeshifting is more powerful. You can cast illusory disguise as an innate occult cantrip at will.

feats.name: Belly Drum
feats.description: Once per day, you can cast fear as an innate primal spell by drumming on your belly.

feats.name: Tanuki Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Deception and Performance. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Lucky Charm
feats.description: Once per day when an adjacent ally fails a check, they can reroll it and use the better result.

feats.name: Sake Belly
feats.description: Once per day, you produce a flask of magical sake. A creature that drinks it gains a +1 circumstance bonus to attacks and saves against fear for 1 hour.

feats.name: Full Transform
feats.description: Once per day, you can cast nature form as an innate primal spell.

vanara.name: Vanara

special.name: Prehensile Tail
special.description: Your tail can be used to hold items and perform simple tasks. You can Interact with objects using your tail as if it were a hand, leaving your other hands free.

vanara.description: Vanaras are intelligent, monkey-like humanoids with prehensile tails and exceptional agility. Found throughout tropical and subtropical regions, they are known for quick wits, spiritual traditions, and formidable martial arts. Their communities are lively and community-oriented.

heritages.name: Jungle Vanara
heritages.description: You gain a climb Speed of 20 feet and do not treat branches and vines as difficult terrain.

heritages.name: Mountain Vanara
heritages.description: You ignore difficult terrain from rocky surfaces and gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Athletics checks to Climb.

heritages.name: River Vanara
heritages.description: You gain a swim Speed of 20 feet and a +2 circumstance bonus to Athletics checks to swim.

heritages.name: Warrior Vanara
heritages.description: Your unarmed Strikes deal 1d6 bludgeoning damage and have the agile and finesse traits.

feats.name: Tree Runner
feats.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Acrobatics and Athletics checks in arboreal environments.

feats.name: Vanara Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Acrobatics and Athletics. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Tail Strike
feats.description: You gain a tail unarmed attack dealing 1d4 bludgeoning damage with the agile, finesse, and trip traits.

feats.name: Brachiation
feats.description: When moving through arboreal environments, you can move at full Speed even while Climbing.

feats.name: Monkey Grip
feats.description: You cannot be disarmed and gain a +4 circumstance bonus to checks to maintain a grapple.

wayang.name: Wayang

senses.name: Darkvision
senses.description: You can see in darkness and dim light just as well as you can see in bright light, though your vision in darkness is in black and white.

special.name: Shadow Connection
special.description: Your connection to shadow grants you a +1 circumstance bonus to saves against shadow and darkness effects.

wayang.description: Wayangs are small, shadow-touched humanoids who have lived in harmony with the Shadow Plane for generations. Mysterious and secretive, their culture revolves around shadow puppet performances they believe can influence reality itself.

heritages.name: Dissolution Wayang
heritages.description: Once per day, you can become invisible as a free action for 1 minute.

heritages.name: Shadow Weaver Wayang
heritages.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to spell attack rolls and DCs for shadow and darkness spells.

heritages.name: Street Performer Wayang
heritages.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Performance checks and can cast ghost sound as an innate occult cantrip at will.

feats.name: Wayang Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Occultism and Performance. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Shadow Magic
feats.description: You can cast shadow trap as an innate occult cantrip at will.

feats.name: Puppet Master
feats.description: Once per round as a free action, you create a shadow puppet that gives one enemy within 30 feet a -1 circumstance penalty to Perception until the start of your next turn.

feats.name: Shadowstep
feats.description: Once per day, you can teleport up to 30 feet to a location in dim light or darkness as a single action.

feats.name: Master of Shadow
feats.description: You can cast darkness at will as an innate occult spell.

anadi.name: Anadi

special.name: Spider Form
special.description: You can shift between humanoid and spider form as a single action. In spider form you gain a climb Speed of 20 feet and a venomous fangs unarmed attack dealing 1d6 piercing plus 1d4 poison damage.

special.name: Anadi Venom
special.description: Creatures you bite must succeed at a Fortitude save against your class DC or take 1d4 persistent poison damage.

anadi.description: Anadi are intelligent, humanoid spiders who live in the Mwangi Expanse. Skilled at disguise and illusion, they often use their shapeshifting ability to appear as other humanoids. Their twin forms stem from carefully developed magic.

heritages.name: Adaptive Anadi
heritages.description: You gain trained in one additional skill and one additional language.

heritages.name: Poisonous Anadi
heritages.description: Your venom is particularly potent. The persistent poison damage from your fangs increases to 1d6.

heritages.name: Studious Anadi
heritages.description: You gain trained in Crafting and can create and read complex webs as coded text.

heritages.name: Venomous Anadi
heritages.description: On a critical hit with your fangs, the target is also enfeebled 1 for 1 round.

feats.name: Anadi Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Deception and Stealth. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Web Spinner
feats.description: You can produce silk to create rope up to 50 feet per day, or web traps as snares.

feats.name: Illusory Disguise
feats.description: You can cast illusory disguise as an innate occult cantrip at will.

feats.name: Spider Climb
feats.description: You gain a climb Speed of 20 feet in both your humanoid and spider forms.

feats.name: Perfect Mimicry
feats.description: You can cast 5th-level illusory disguise as an innate occult spell once per day.

android.name: Android

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

special.name: Constructed
special.description: You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saves against diseases, poisons, and radiation. You do not need to breathe.

special.name: Emotionally Unaware
special.description: You take a -1 circumstance penalty to Diplomacy and Performance checks, and to Perception checks to Sense Motive.

android.description: Androids are humanoids constructed of living metal and powered by an internal energy source. Created by a highly advanced society, many androids seek to understand what it means to be alive and to forge their own identities.

heritages.name: Chassis Android
heritages.description: Your chassis is built for durability. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC when unarmored.

heritages.name: Laborer Android
heritages.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Athletics checks and your carrying capacity increases by 2 Bulk.

heritages.name: Polyglot Android
heritages.description: Your language banks are extensive. You gain two additional languages of your choice.

heritages.name: Warrior Android
heritages.description: Combat protocols are deeply embedded in your programming. You are trained in light and medium armor.

feats.name: Android Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Crafting and Society. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Efficient Upgrades
feats.description: Once per day during daily preparations, you can gain a single skill feat for which you meet the prerequisites until your next daily preparations.

feats.name: Nanite Surge
feats.description: Once per day as a free action, you can heal 1d8 HP per level, but become stunned 1 until the start of your next turn.

feats.name: Targeting Subroutine
feats.description: Once per round, you can ignore the flat-footed bonus AC against one of your attacks.

feats.name: Override Inhibitors
feats.description: Once per day, you can gain a second action this turn, but become stunned 1 at the start of your next turn.

automaton.name: Automaton

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

senses.name: Arcane Sense
senses.description: Your core grants you an imprecise arcane sense with a range of 30 feet, detecting magical auras.

special.name: Constructed Body
special.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saves against disease, poison, and sleep effects. You do not breathe, eat, or drink.

special.name: Arcane Core
special.description: You can cast a cantrip from the arcane spell list as an innate arcane cantrip at will.

automaton.description: Automatons are mechanical constructs built by the ancient Jistka Imperium and powered by a spark of life called an arcane core. After millennia, some have developed true consciousness and seek to reclaim their past and build new futures.

heritages.name: Artillerist Automaton
heritages.description: You have an integrated ranged weapon dealing 1d6 energy damage of a type you choose at creation, with a range of 60 feet.

heritages.name: Mage Automaton
heritages.description: You gain a 1st-rank arcane spell as a once-per-day innate arcane spell.

heritages.name: Scout Automaton
heritages.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Stealth and Perception checks.

heritages.name: Warrior Automaton
heritages.description: Your unarmed attacks deal 1d8 bludgeoning damage and you gain the Attack of Opportunity reaction.

feats.name: Automaton Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Arcana and Crafting. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Ceaseless Recollections
feats.description: Once per day, you can use Recall Knowledge even without training in the relevant skill.

feats.name: Inscribed Core
feats.description: You can cast an additional cantrip from the arcane spell list at will.

feats.name: Energy Beam
feats.description: Once per round, you can fire an energy ray dealing 1d8 damage of your chosen energy type with a range of 60 feet.

feats.name: Self-Repair
feats.description: Once per day as a single action, you can heal yourself for 2d8 HP per level.

conrasu.name: Conrasu

special.name: Sunlight Healing
special.description: You can enter a meditative, healing state as a 10-minute activity when exposed to direct sunlight, recovering 1d8 HP (plus 1d8 per 2 levels after 1st). Once done, you are immune to this for 1 day.

conrasu.description: Conrasue are rigid, armored plant creatures connected to the cosmic order of the universe. Animated by shards of cosmic force, they believe in maintaining balance. Though strange and alien in appearance, conrasue are thoughtful beings who cherish their community.

heritages.name: Maintainer Conrasu
heritages.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Nature checks relating to plants and a constant speak with plants effect.

heritages.name: Repairer Conrasu
heritages.description: Your cosmic energy can restore construct-like beings. You can cast mending as an innate primal spell at will.

heritages.name: Templar Conrasu
heritages.description: You are a warrior of cosmic order. You are trained in martial weapons and gain the Armor Proficiency general feat.

feats.name: Conrasu Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Nature and Religion. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Hardened Shell
feats.description: Your outer shell is especially dense. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC and to checks to resist forced movement.

feats.name: Spellbound Shell
feats.description: Once per day, you can cast mage armor as an innate primal spell.

feats.name: Wood Stride
feats.description: You are unaffected by difficult terrain from plants and undergrowth.

feats.name: Cosmic Order
feats.description: Once per day, you can negate one chaos-typed spell or effect as a reaction.

dragonet.name: Dragonet

senses.name: Darkvision
senses.description: You can see in darkness and dim light just as well as you can see in bright light, though your vision in darkness is in black and white.

senses.name: Scent
senses.description: You have an imprecise scent sense with a range of 30 feet.

special.name: Draconic Wings
special.description: You have functional wings. You gain a fly Speed of 20 feet.

dragonet.description: Dragonets are miniature dragons, complete with wings, dagger-like teeth, and magical breath. They can be fiercely independent and proud, at times protective or avaricious, and they delight in the finer things in life.

heritages.name: Chromatic Dragonet
heritages.description: Choose one energy type: acid, cold, electricity, fire, or poison. You gain resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1) and can breathe a 15-foot cone of that energy once per day.

heritages.name: Metallic Dragonet
heritages.description: Choose one energy type: acid, cold, electricity, or fire. You gain resistance and can breathe a 15-foot cone of that energy once per day.

heritages.name: Primal Dragonet
heritages.description: Your draconic magic is especially strong. You can cast a cantrip from the arcane spell list as an innate arcane cantrip at will.

feats.name: Draconic Claws
feats.description: You gain a claw unarmed attack dealing 1d4 slashing damage with the agile and finesse traits.

feats.name: Dragonet Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Arcana and Intimidation. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Terrifying Presence
feats.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Intimidation checks and can Demoralize creatures up to 60 feet away.

feats.name: Breath Weapon
feats.description: You can use your draconic breath weapon twice per day and it now deals 2d6 damage.

feats.name: True Draconic Form
feats.description: Once per day, you can cast dragon form as an innate arcane spell.

fleshwarp.name: Fleshwarp

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

special.name: Unusual Anatomy
special.description: Your strange body resists confinement. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Fortitude saves and checks to Escape grapples, and can attempt to Escape as a free action once per round.

fleshwarp.description: Fleshwarps are creatures whose humanoid ancestors were subject to powerful magical transformations. Their strange forms reflect the magical forces that reshaped them. Each fleshwarp is unique and finds strength in their remarkable resilience.

heritages.name: Irongut Fleshwarp
heritages.description: You can eat non-food items for nourishment and gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saves against ingested afflictions.

heritages.name: Phantasmal Fleshwarp
heritages.description: Part of your form shifts between planes. You can Hide even while observed and gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Stealth checks.

heritages.name: Spinewarden Fleshwarp
heritages.description: You have bony protrusions. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC, and when a creature strikes or grapples you, they take 1d4 piercing damage.

feats.name: Fleshwarp Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Medicine and Occultism. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Mutate
feats.description: Once per day as a single action, you can gain a swim speed of 20 feet, a climb speed of 20 feet, or low-light vision for 1 hour.

feats.name: Aberrant Resistance
feats.description: You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saves against transmutation effects and effects that would change your shape.

feats.name: Unnatural Resilience
feats.description: When you recover HP from any source, add your Constitution modifier to the amount recovered.

feats.name: Shapeshifter's Edge
feats.description: Once per round, you can change your fleshwarp heritage ability as a free action.

ghoran.name: Ghoran

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

special.name: Seed
special.description: You produce a seed. Upon your death, the seed can be planted to eventually produce a new ghoran with similar but altered memories, considered a separate individual.
