ghoran.description: Ghorans are plant-like humanoids created by an ancient druidic sect to preserve memories across generations. Their bodies are composed of plant matter and wood, and each ghoran carries the faint experiences of previous ghorans through inherited memory.

heritages.name: Photosynthetic Ghoran
heritages.description: Once per day when exposed to direct sunlight for an hour, you regain HP equal to twice your level.

heritages.name: Thornborn Ghoran
heritages.description: You have thorny protrusions. When a creature strikes you with a melee attack, they take 1d4 piercing damage.

heritages.name: Wildwood Ghoran
heritages.description: You can Hide in natural outdoor environments even without cover.

feats.name: Ghoran Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Nature and Survival. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Verdant Growth
feats.description: Once per day, you can cast entangle as an innate primal spell.

feats.name: Bark Skin
feats.description: Your outer layer is like bark. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC when unarmored.

feats.name: Fruit Body
feats.description: Once per day, you produce a fruit that can be eaten as a single action to heal 2d8 HP.

feats.name: Rootwalker
feats.description: You gain tremorsense (imprecise, 30 feet) while in contact with the ground.

goloma.name: Goloma

senses.name: Eight Eyes
senses.description: Your eight eyes give you a wider field of vision. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks and cannot be flanked.

goloma.description: Golomas are four-armed humanoids with eight eyes that grant them nearly 360-degree vision. Defined by their vigilance and deep-seated wariness of predators, golomas are excellent hunters and trackers who have built their culture around protection and awareness.

heritages.name: Fearful Goloma
heritages.description: Your vigilance extends to sensing danger. You gain the Danger Sense skill feat for free.

heritages.name: Night Goloma
heritages.description: You have adapted to hunting at night. You gain darkvision.

heritages.name: Wilderness Goloma
heritages.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Survival checks to Track and can Track at your full Speed.

feats.name: Four-Armed Hunter
feats.description: Your four arms let you hold an additional weapon or item and perform actions requiring two hands with three arms.

feats.name: Goloma Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Nature and Survival. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Reactive Eyes
feats.description: Your eight eyes react to danger. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Reflex saves and initiative checks.

feats.name: Many-Handed Assault
feats.description: Once per round, you can make one additional unarmed Strike as a free action.

feats.name: All-Seeing Vigilance
feats.description: You gain a constant true seeing innate primal effect.

jotunborn.name: Jotunborn

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

special.name: Iivlar Weaving
special.description: Planar thread produced by creatures called iivlars is woven into your skin. This thread glows with dim light in a 10-foot emanation. You can Sustain to extinguish, reactivate, or adjust the coloration of this light.

jotunborn.description: Jotunborn were forged from the blood of an ancient group of giants called titans, tasked to watch over the Universe. These massive humanoids carry the power of their giant ancestors in their blood and serve as stewards of the natural world.

heritages.name: Cloud Jotunborn
heritages.description: You carry storm giant blood. You gain electricity resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1) and can cast gust of wind as an innate primal spell once per day.

heritages.name: Fire Jotunborn
heritages.description: You carry fire giant blood. You gain fire resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1) and your unarmed Strikes deal an extra 1d4 fire damage.

heritages.name: Frost Jotunborn
heritages.description: You carry frost giant blood. You gain cold resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1) and your unarmed Strikes deal an extra 1d4 cold damage.

heritages.name: Stone Jotunborn
heritages.description: You carry stone giant blood. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Fortitude saves and your unarmed Strikes deal an extra 1d4 bludgeoning damage.

feats.name: Giant's Throw
feats.description: You can throw rocks or boulders as a ranged attack dealing 1d8 bludgeoning damage with a range of 60 feet.

feats.name: Jotunborn Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Athletics and Intimidation. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Powerful Build
feats.description: You count as one size larger for carrying capacity and which creatures you can grapple or Shove.

feats.name: Rend
feats.description: When you successfully Grapple a creature, you also deal 1d6 bludgeoning damage to them as part of the same action.

feats.name: Giant's Fury
feats.description: Once per round when you hit with an unarmed Strike, you can push the target up to 10 feet away.

kashrishi.name: Kashrishi

senses.name: Empathic Sense
senses.description: Kashrishi get a vague sense of the current emotional state of others within 15 feet of them, a general feeling of happiness, anger, or fear.

special.name: Crystal Horn
special.description: You have a natural horn that can focus psychic energy. Your horn unarmed attack deals 1d6 piercing damage.

kashrishi.description: Kashrishi are small, rhinoceros-like humanoids with crystalline horns that grant them psychic abilities. They are perceptive and thoughtful beings who sense the mental states of those around them. Their society values wisdom and mental discipline above all.

heritages.name: Crystal-Bright Kashrishi
heritages.description: Your horn shines with inner light. You can activate your horn to produce bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light in a 40-foot radius as a free action.

heritages.name: Focused Kashrishi
heritages.description: Your mental focus is exceptional. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Will saves and to Perception checks to Sense Motive.

heritages.name: Resonant Kashrishi
heritages.description: Your horn resonates with psychic energy. You can cast detect thoughts as an innate occult spell once per day.

feats.name: Kashrishi Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Diplomacy and Occultism. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Mental Shield
feats.description: Once per round as a reaction, you can gain a +2 circumstance bonus to a Will save against a mental effect.

feats.name: Expanded Thoughtsense
feats.description: Your empathic sense range increases to 60 feet.

feats.name: Mind Blast
feats.description: Once per day, you can cast mind probe as an innate occult spell.

feats.name: Psychic Aura
feats.description: Enemies within 10 feet of you take a -1 circumstance penalty to Will saves.

poppet.name: Poppet

senses.name: Darkvision
senses.description: You can see in darkness and dim light just as well as you can see in bright light, though your vision in darkness is in black and white.

special.name: Constructed
special.description: Your constructed body resists ailments that assail the flesh. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against death effects, disease, and poison, and against effects that would give you the drained, paralyzed, or sickened conditions.

poppet.description: Poppets are small animated objects that have gained sapience and a spark of life. Born from beloved toys or crafted by caring hands, each poppet is unique. They often seek to understand the world that created them and find their place among living creatures.

heritages.name: Ghost Poppet
heritages.description: You are partially incorporeal. Once per day, you can move through a solid object up to 5 feet thick.

heritages.name: Toy Poppet
heritages.description: You were once a beloved toy. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Deception and Performance checks when pretending to be an inanimate object.

heritages.name: Vintage Poppet
heritages.description: You are an old creation filled with absorbed experience. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to all untrained skill checks.

heritages.name: Warrior Poppet
heritages.description: You were crafted to fight. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls with unarmed attacks.

feats.name: Button Eyes
feats.description: Your button eyes are surprisingly perceptive. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to find hidden objects and secret doors.

feats.name: Detachable Head
feats.description: You can remove your head. Your detached head can speak and perceive surroundings within 30 feet of your body.

feats.name: Poppet Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Crafting and Deception. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Cute Nod
feats.description: Your childlike appearance causes others to lower their guard. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Diplomacy checks to Make an Impression.

feats.name: Live Again
feats.description: Once per day, when reduced to 0 HP, you remain at 1 HP and gain the quickened condition for 1 round.

sarangay.name: Sarangay

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

special.name: Sacred Jewel
special.description: You carry a sacred jewel in your brow that houses your soul. If you die, your jewel can be used in a week-long ritual to restore you to life without material cost.

special.name: Horns
special.description: You have powerful horns. You gain a horns unarmed attack dealing 1d8 piercing damage.

sarangay.description: Sarangays are powerful, carabao-headed humanoids from Tian Xia who bear sacred jewels in their brows. They are strong warriors with a deep connection to the spirit world. Their jewels are believed to contain their souls, granting them spiritual resilience.

heritages.name: Forest Sarangay
heritages.description: You ignore difficult terrain from undergrowth and gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Survival checks in forested areas.

heritages.name: Spirit-Touched Sarangay
heritages.description: Your jewel has a strong connection to the spirit world. You can see invisible spirits and gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Religion checks.

heritages.name: War-Scarred Sarangay
heritages.description: Your body is covered in battle scars that have toughened your skin. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your AC.

feats.name: Sarangay Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Athletics and Religion. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Goring Charge
feats.description: When you Stride at least 10 feet and then Strike with your horns, deal an extra 1d6 damage and attempt to Trip the target.

feats.name: Jewel Guardian
feats.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saves against death effects and effects that would reduce your maximum HP.

feats.name: Ancestral Rage
feats.description: Once per day, you can enter a rage granting a +2 circumstance bonus to attack and damage rolls for 1 minute.

feats.name: Soul Fortification
feats.description: Your sacred jewel provides extraordinary protection. You become immune to death effects and spells that target your soul.

shisk.name: Shisk

senses.name: Darkvision
senses.description: You can see in darkness and dim light just as well as you can see in bright light, though your vision in darkness is in black and white.

special.name: Spine Defense
special.description: Your body is covered in sharp spines. When a creature hits you with an unarmed attack or natural weapon, they take 1d4 piercing damage.

shisk.description: Shisks are porcupine-like humanoids renowned as keepers of secret knowledge. They are reclusive scholars who jealously guard lore that other peoples have forgotten. Their spine-covered bodies make them formidable even without weapons.

heritages.name: Spine Hurler Shisk
heritages.description: You can launch your spines as projectiles. You gain a spine ranged unarmed attack dealing 1d6 piercing damage with a range increment of 30 feet.

heritages.name: Tattoo-Shisk
heritages.description: You have ritual tattoos that protect you. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC and to saves against magical effects.

heritages.name: Twilight Shisk
heritages.description: You have adapted to dim twilight zones. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Stealth checks in dim light.

feats.name: Ancient Memories
feats.description: Once per day, you can attempt a Recall Knowledge check as if trained in the relevant skill, even if you are untrained.

feats.name: Shisk Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in two Lore skills of your choice.

feats.name: Quill Barrage
feats.description: Once per day, you can shoot spines in a 15-foot cone dealing 2d6 piercing damage (basic Reflex save against your class DC).

feats.name: Secret Keeper
feats.description: Once per day, you can use ancient knowledge to gain a +4 circumstance bonus to one Recall Knowledge check.

feats.name: Master of Secrets
feats.description: You gain legendary proficiency in one Lore skill of your choice.

shoony.name: Shoony

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

special.name: Blunt Snout
special.description: Your snout gives you an excellent sense of smell. You gain the scent sense (imprecise, 30 feet).

shoony.description: Shoonies are small, dog-like humanoids with a deep love of peace, community, and simple pleasures. Despite their gentle nature, shoonies can be fierce defenders of their communities. They are excellent farmers, builders, and craftspeople who prefer to resolve conflicts peacefully.

heritages.name: Fisherkith Shoony
heritages.description: You gain a swim Speed of 15 feet and a +2 circumstance bonus to Athletics checks to swim.

heritages.name: Paddler Shoony
heritages.description: Your webbed paws make you an excellent swimmer. You gain a swim Speed of 20 feet.

heritages.name: Thickpaw Shoony
heritages.description: Your paws are thick and callused. You gain a claws unarmed attack dealing 1d4 slashing damage with the agile, finesse, and unarmed traits.

feats.name: Canine Cadence
feats.description: Your cheerful nature is infectious. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Diplomacy checks to Make an Impression on any non-hostile creature.

feats.name: Shoony Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Athletics and Crafting. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Shoony Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You are trained with the crossbow, handaxe, sickle, and all shoony weapons.

feats.name: Dig Down
feats.description: You gain a burrow Speed of 10 feet through earth (but not stone).

feats.name: Loyal Retreat
feats.description: When you use the Aid reaction, you can also move up to 10 feet before aiding.

skeleton.name: Skeleton

senses.name: Darkvision
senses.description: You can see in darkness and dim light just as well as you can see in bright light, though your vision in darkness is in black and white.

special.name: Undead Nature
special.description: You do not need to eat or drink, but you do need to sleep. Negative energy heals you; positive energy harms you.

special.name: Rattling Death
special.description: When you take bludgeoning damage, you can release a rattling sound as a free action that can Demoralize one creature within 30 feet.

skeleton.description: Skeletons are undead creatures animated by necromantic magic. Some retain memories and personalities from their past lives; others are newly awakened spirits finding their way in an unfamiliar bony form. Skeleton adventurers often seek to understand their undead existence.

heritages.name: Bone Skipper Skeleton
heritages.description: Your joints are especially nimble. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Acrobatics checks and can squeeze through tight spaces as if they were normal terrain.

heritages.name: Fodder Skeleton
heritages.description: Once per day as a single action, you can regain 1d6 HP per level.

heritages.name: Sturdy Skeleton
heritages.description: Your bones are unusually dense. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Fortitude DC against combat maneuvers and a +1 circumstance bonus to your AC.

heritages.name: Skulking Skeleton
heritages.description: You move silently for a skeleton. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Stealth checks.

feats.name: Clatter
feats.description: Once per round, you can Demoralize a creature using the rattle of your bones as a free action.

feats.name: Marrow Storage
feats.description: You can store items with a total Bulk of 1 inside your hollow skeleton frame.

feats.name: Skeleton Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Medicine and Religion. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Ossuary Path
feats.description: Once per day, you can move through solid objects up to 5 feet thick, emerging in an open space.

feats.name: Reassemble
feats.description: Once per day, when reduced to 0 HP, your bones scatter and reform within 30 feet with 1 HP.

sprite.name: Sprite

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

special.name: Fey Influence
special.description: Your fey nature gives you a connection to the supernatural world. You can cast detect magic as an innate primal cantrip at will.

sprite.description: Sprites are tiny faeries with a strong connection to the First World. Despite their small size, sprites are fierce and capable adventurers who compensate with magic and agility. They can fly and carry an innate magical nature that makes them valuable allies.

heritages.name: Draxie Sprite
heritages.description: You have innate draconic magic. You can cast a cantrip from the arcane spell list as an innate arcane cantrip at will.

heritages.name: Grig Sprite
heritages.description: You have a beautiful singing voice. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Performance checks and can cast inspire courage as an innate occult cantrip once per day.

heritages.name: Melixie Sprite
heritages.description: You have a bee-like body. You gain a stinger unarmed attack dealing 1d4 piercing plus 1d4 poison damage.

heritages.name: Nixie Sprite
heritages.description: You are a water sprite. You gain a swim Speed of 20 feet and can breathe underwater.

heritages.name: Nyktera Sprite
heritages.description: You are a night sprite. You gain darkvision and can cast darkness as an innate occult spell once per day.

heritages.name: Pixie Sprite
heritages.description: You have pixie wings. You gain a fly Speed of 20 feet.

feats.name: Fey Dust
feats.description: Once per day, you can cast glitterdust as an innate primal spell.

feats.name: Sprite Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Nature and Occultism. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Sprite Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You are trained with all Tiny and Small weapons and can use any of them one-handed.

feats.name: Invisibility Veil
feats.description: Once per day, you can cast invisibility on yourself as an innate primal spell.

feats.name: Fey Charm
feats.description: Once per day, you can cast charm as a 3rd-rank innate primal spell.

strix.name: Strix

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

special.name: Strix Wings
special.description: You have powerful wings. You can Fly at a Speed of 20 feet. Wearing medium or heavy armor may limit your flight at the GM discretion.

strix.description: Strix are a reclusive avian people who live in isolated mountain communities. Paranoid by nature due to historical persecution, strix are cautious about outsiders but fiercely loyal to those who earn their trust. They are excellent hunters with powerful wings that grant them flight.

heritages.name: Aerial Strix
heritages.description: You are an exceptional flier. Your fly Speed increases to 30 feet and you can Hover without spending actions.

heritages.name: Nightglider Strix
heritages.description: You are most comfortable in the dark. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Stealth checks at night or in dim light.

heritages.name: Predator Strix
heritages.description: You hunt with fierce instincts. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Survival checks to Track.

heritages.name: Shoreline Strix
heritages.description: Your people live near the coast. You gain a swim Speed of 20 feet.

heritages.name: Windborn Strix
heritages.description: The wind is your ally. You ignore wind-based penalties to movement and flight.

feats.name: Strix Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Acrobatics and Stealth. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Talon Strike
feats.description: You gain a talon unarmed attack dealing 1d4 slashing damage with the agile, finesse, and unarmed traits.

feats.name: Vigilance
feats.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to notice hidden creatures approaching from the air.

feats.name: Diving Strike
feats.description: When you Fly and then Strike a creature below you, you deal an extra 1d6 damage.

feats.name: Ferocious Beaks
feats.description: Your beak unarmed attack gains the forceful trait.

feats.name: Great Strix Wings
feats.description: Your fly Speed increases by 10 feet and you can carry a Medium or smaller creature while flying.

surki.name: Surki

senses.name: Darkvision
senses.description: You can see in darkness and dim light just as well as you can see in bright light, though your vision in darkness is in black and white.

special.name: Silk Spinner
special.description: You can produce silk from glands in your wrists, using it to create simple tools, climb as if you had a climb Speed of 20 feet, or entangle foes.

surki.description: Surkis are insectile humanoids who subsist on the latent magic in the world around them. As a subterranean species native to the Darklands, surkis have rarely been seen on the surface, only now emerging in a generational dig. Each surki is unique, with adaptations reflecting their adventures and chosen role.

heritages.name: Ambush Surki
heritages.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Stealth checks when in areas with webs or silk coverings.

heritages.name: Burrowing Surki
heritages.description: You can burrow through earth. You gain a burrow Speed of 15 feet through earth (not stone).

heritages.name: Venomous Surki
heritages.description: Your bite carries venom. You gain a fangs unarmed attack dealing 1d6 piercing plus 1d4 poison damage.

feats.name: Surki Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Crafting and Nature. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Web Trap
feats.description: Once per day, you can create a web trap as a 1-minute activity that functions as a snare.

feats.name: Spindle Silk
feats.description: Your silk is particularly strong. You can use your silk to Grapple a creature up to 30 feet away as a ranged attack.

feats.name: Web Highway
feats.description: Once per day, you can create a 60-foot silk highway that you and allies can Stride across at full Speed, even vertically.

feats.name: Web Master
feats.description: Once per day, you can create web structures that entangle all creatures in a 20-foot square as a 2-action activity.

vishkanya.name: Vishkanya

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

special.name: Venomous
special.description: Your blood and saliva carry a natural venom. Once per day, you can envenom a weapon or your unarmed attack as a free action, dealing 1d4 poison damage and 1d4 persistent poison damage on a hit.

vishkanya.description: Vishkanyas are ophidian humanoids who carry potent venom within their blood and saliva. Graceful and deadly, with scaly skin and slit pupils hinting at their serpentine heritage. Though often feared for their venom, vishkanyas value harmony and community as much as any other people.

heritages.name: Keen-Nosed Vishkanya
heritages.description: Your sense of smell is exceptionally acute. You gain the scent sense (imprecise, 30 feet) and a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks relying on smell.

heritages.name: Selective Venom Vishkanya
heritages.description: You have fine control over your venom. You can choose who is affected by your venom and can adjust its potency as a free action.

heritages.name: Slithery Vishkanya
heritages.description: Your serpentine flexibility is exceptional. You can squeeze through tight spaces as if they were normal terrain.

heritages.name: Nimble Vishkanya
heritages.description: You are particularly agile. You gain a +5-foot status bonus to your Speed.

feats.name: Vishkanya Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Acrobatics and Stealth. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Toxic Secretions
feats.description: Your skin secretes natural toxin. Creatures that grapple you or that you grapple take poison damage from your venom.

feats.name: Serpentine Flexibility
feats.description: You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Escape checks and to checks to squeeze through narrow spaces.

feats.name: Cobra's Swiftness
feats.description: Once per round, you can make one additional unarmed Strike as a free action.

feats.name: Improved Venom
feats.description: Your natural venom now deals 2d4 poison damage and 2d4 persistent poison damage.

yaksha.name: Yaksha

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

special.name: Spirit Connection
special.description: You have a connection to the spirit world. You can see spiritual presences invisible to others and can detect haunt-type hazards within 30 feet automatically.

yaksha.description: Yakshas are divine guardian spirits who emigrated to Golarion from the First World. Famed for resolute vows and prowess with primal magic, yakshas shield the indigent and protect the wilderness, punishing those who threaten either with bone-crushing fury.

heritages.name: Bountiful Yaksha
heritages.description: You are a yaksha of prosperity and abundance. You can cast create food as an innate primal spell once per day.

heritages.name: Fierce Yaksha
heritages.description: You are a warrior yaksha. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to attack rolls on your first Strike each turn.

heritages.name: Guardian Yaksha
heritages.description: You protect sacred sites. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks and cannot be surprised.

feats.name: Spirit Sight
feats.description: Your spiritual vision is enhanced. You can see invisible undead and spirits within 30 feet.

feats.name: Yaksha Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Nature and Religion. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Spiritual Weapon
feats.description: Once per day, you can cast spirit explosion as an innate primal spell.

feats.name: Ward the Sacred
feats.description: Once per day, you can create a 30-foot sanctified zone lasting 1 hour where evil spirits cannot enter.

feats.name: Spirit Form
feats.description: Once per day, you can become incorporeal for 1 minute.

yaoguai.name: Yaoguai

senses.name: Low-Light Vision
senses.description: You can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so you ignore the concealed condition due to dim light.

special.name: Change Shape
special.description: You can shift between your humanoid and true yaoguai form as a single action. This form can resemble any common Medium humanoid ancestry prevalent where you were born. You can remain in humanoid form indefinitely, but revert after 1 hour of unconsciousness.

yaoguai.description: Yaoguai are spirit-touched shapeshifters from Tian Xia, descended from animals, plants, or objects who gained spiritual power and humanoid form through cultivation. Each yaoguai is associated with a specific origin that influences their abilities and personality. They are known as tricksters and wanderers.

heritages.name: Fox Yaoguai
heritages.description: You have fox heritage. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Deception and Stealth checks.

heritages.name: Serpent Yaoguai
heritages.description: You have serpent heritage. You gain darkvision and resistance to poison equal to half your level (minimum 1).

heritages.name: Tiger Yaoguai
heritages.description: You have tiger heritage. You gain a claws unarmed attack dealing 1d6 slashing damage and a +2 circumstance bonus to Athletics checks to Climb.

heritages.name: Tortoise Yaoguai
heritages.description: You have tortoise heritage. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Fortitude DC and saves against effects that would move you or knock you prone.

feats.name: Animal Transformation
feats.description: Your shapeshifting is more developed. You can assume your animal form as a single action and maintain it indefinitely.

feats.name: Yaoguai Lore
feats.description: You gain trained in Deception and Nature. If you would automatically become trained in one, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice.

feats.name: Spirit Trickery
feats.description: You can cast ghost sound as an innate occult cantrip at will.

feats.name: Accumulated Power
feats.description: The spiritual power you have cultivated grants you an additional use of your ancestry spell per day.

feats.name: Perfect Transformation
feats.description: You can now transform into any animal of your size category or smaller at will.
